revision.comment: 新しいページ：姥塚古墳（山梨県笛吹市にある古墳）。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 姥塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 御場塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上941
（南照院境内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 4.95 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 39|経度秒 = 29.85 |ISO = JP-19 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径40m
高さ10m |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 山梨県指定史跡「姥塚」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=姥塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''姥塚古墳'''（うばづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「姥塚」）。 == 概要 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南照院本堂（右）・姥塚古墳（左奥）}}]] [[甲府盆地]]南東部、金川扇状地の左岸扇端部に築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。現在は慈眼山南照院境内に位置し、石室内には聖観音像が祀られる。これまでに測量調査のほか、南側周溝部の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。主体部の埋葬施設は右片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。現存石室長17.54メートルを測る非常に大型の石室であり、山梨県では最大規模かつ東日本でも屈指の規模になる。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 この姥塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末葉{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}（または6世紀後半）頃の築造と推定される。南側には同時期の集落遺跡として二之宮・姥塚遺跡が広がる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。甲斐地方では古墳時代前期に[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[甲府市]]下曽根町）が築造されて以降、中期に大型古墳は築造されていないが、後期には本古墳のほか[[加牟那塚古墳]]・[[万寿森古墳]]（いずれも甲府市千塚）といった大型石室墳が築造されており、特殊な様相を呈する{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。なお伝承では、山姥が大男と競争して造ったため「姥塚」といい、[[聖徳太子]]が甲斐に入ったときに愛馬が倒れて葬ったので「御馬塚（おんばづか）」というとする史跡説明板。。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に山梨県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）5月13日、山梨県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、墳丘・石室の測量調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、南照院庫裏建設に伴う南側周溝部の発掘調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては右片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * 石室全長：17.54メートル（現存部） * 玄室：長さ9メートル、幅3.3メートル（奥壁部）・3メートル（玄門部）、高さ4.2メートル * 羨道：幅2.4メートル（玄門部）、高さ2.5メートル 石室の石材は1-1.5メートルの自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室の奥壁は2段積みで、下段は2枚組、上段は1枚石からなる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。天井石は玄室部で5枚、羨道部で4枚（開口部の1枚は崩落）{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室と羨道の境は、幅0.9メートル・高さ約2メートルの方柱状柱石で区画される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 なお、南照院境内には大型の石材が点在することから、羨道はさらに5-6メートル長くなるとする説がある{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 姥塚 - 1965年（昭和40年）5月13日指定[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚]（山梨県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html 姥塚【県指定】]（笛吹市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（山梨県教育委員会・御坂町教育委員会・慈眼山南照院設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=原田道雄|year=1989|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 19 山梨県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490190|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[加牟那塚古墳]] * [[万寿森古墳]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:うはつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

主体部の埋葬施設としては右片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * 石室全長：17.54メートル（現存部） * 玄室：長さ9メートル、幅3.3メートル（奥壁部）・3メートル（玄門部）、高さ4.2メートル * 羨道：幅2.4メートル（玄門部）、高さ2.5メートル 石室の石材は1-1.5メートルの自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室の奥壁は2段積みで、下段は2枚組、上段は1枚石からなる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。天井石は玄室部で5枚、羨道部で4枚（開口部の1枚は崩落）{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室と羨道の境は、幅0.9メートル・高さ約2メートルの方柱状柱石で区画される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 なお、南照院境内には大型の石材が点在することから、羨道はさらに5-6メートル長くなるとする説がある{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 姥塚 - 1965年（昭和40年）5月13日指定[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚]（山梨県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html 姥塚【県指定】]（笛吹市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（山梨県教育委員会・御坂町教育委員会・慈眼山南照院設置） * {{Cite 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:うはつかこふん}} {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 姥塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 御場塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上941
（南照院境内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 4.95 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 39|経度秒 = 29.85 |ISO = JP-19 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径40m
高さ10m |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 山梨県指定史跡「姥塚」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=姥塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''姥塚古墳'''（うばづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「姥塚」）。 == 概要 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南照院本堂（右）・姥塚古墳（左奥）}}]] [[甲府盆地]]南東部、金川扇状地の左岸扇端部に築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。現在は慈眼山南照院境内に位置し、石室内には聖観音像が祀られる。これまでに測量調査のほか、南側周溝部の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。主体部の埋葬施設は右片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。現存石室長17.54メートルを測る非常に大型の石室であり、山梨県では最大規模かつ東日本でも屈指の規模になる。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 この姥塚古墳は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末葉{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}（または6世紀後半）頃の築造と推定される。南側には同時期の集落遺跡として二之宮・姥塚遺跡が広がる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。甲斐地方では古墳時代前期に[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[甲府市]]下曽根町）が築造されて以降、中期に大型古墳は築造されていないが、後期には本古墳のほか[[加牟那塚古墳]]・[[万寿森古墳]]（いずれも甲府市千塚）といった大型石室墳が築造されており、特殊な様相を呈する{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。なお伝承では、山姥が大男と競争して造ったため「姥塚」といい、[[聖徳太子]]が甲斐に入ったときに愛馬が倒れて葬ったので「御馬塚（おんばづか）」というとする史跡説明板。。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に山梨県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）5月13日、山梨県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、墳丘・石室の測量調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、南照院庫裏建設に伴う南側周溝部の発掘調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室 羨道から玄室を望む}}]] 主体部の埋葬施設としては右片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * 石室全長：17.54メートル（現存部） * 玄室：長さ9メートル、幅3.3メートル（奥壁部）・3メートル（玄門部）、高さ4.2メートル * 羨道：幅2.4メートル（玄門部）、高さ2.5メートル 石室の石材は1-1.5メートルの自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室の奥壁は2段積みで、下段は2枚組、上段は1枚石からなる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。天井石は玄室部で5枚、羨道部で4枚（開口部の1枚は崩落）{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室と羨道の境は、幅0.9メートル・高さ約2メートルの方柱状柱石で区画される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 なお、南照院境内には大型の石材が点在することから、羨道はさらに5-6メートル長くなるとする説がある{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Ubazuka 1965年（昭和40年）5月13日指定[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > {{日本の古墳 |名称 = 姥塚古墳 |画像 = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = 御場塚 |所属 = |所在地 = [[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上941
（南照院境内） |緯度度 = 35|緯度分 = 38|緯度秒 = 4.95 |経度度 =138|経度分 = 39|経度秒 = 29.85 |ISO = JP-19 |形状 = [[円墳]] |規模 = 直径40m
高さ10m |埋葬施設 = 片袖式[[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]末葉 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 山梨県指定史跡「姥塚」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=姥塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.png|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''姥塚古墳'''（うばづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[笛吹市]]御坂町下井之上にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。山梨県指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「姥塚」）。 == 概要 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|南照院本堂（右）・姥塚古墳（左奥）}}]] [[甲府盆地]]南東部、金川扇状地の左岸扇端部に築造された大型円墳である{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。現在は慈眼山南照院境内に位置し、石室内には聖観音像が祀られる。これまでに測量調査のほか、南側周溝部の発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。埋葬施設は右片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。現存石室長17.54メートルを測る非常に大型の石室であり、山梨県では最大規模かつ東日本でも屈指の規模になる。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末葉{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}（または6世紀後半）頃と推定される。南側には同時期の集落遺跡として二之宮・姥塚遺跡が広がる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。甲斐地方では古墳時代前期に[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[甲府市]]下曽根町）が築造されて以降、中期に大型古墳は築造されていないが、後期には本古墳のほか[[加牟那塚古墳]]・[[万寿森古墳]]（いずれも甲府市千塚）といった大型石室墳が築造されており、特殊な様相を呈する{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。なお伝承では、山姥が大男と競争して造ったため「姥塚」といい、[[聖徳太子]]が甲斐に入ったときに愛馬が倒れて葬ったので「御馬塚（おんばづか）」というとする史跡説明板。。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に山梨県指定史跡に指定されている。 === 来歴 === * [[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）5月13日、山梨県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、墳丘・石室の測量調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、南照院庫裏建設に伴う南側周溝部の発掘調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては右片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * 石室全長：17.54メートル（現存部） * 玄室：長さ9メートル、幅3.3メートル（奥壁部）・3メートル（玄門部）、高さ4.2メートル * 羨道：幅2.4メートル（玄門部）、高さ2.5メートル 石室の石材は1-1.5メートルの自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室の奥壁は2段積みで、下段は2枚組、上段は1枚石からなる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。天井石は玄室部で5枚、羨道部で4枚（開口部の1枚は崩落）{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室と羨道の境は、幅0.9メートル・高さ約2メートルの方柱状柱石で区画される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 なお、南照院境内には大型の石材が点在することから、羨道はさらに5-6メートル長くなるとする説がある{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 姥塚 - 1965年（昭和40年）5月13日指定[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚]（山梨県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html 姥塚【県指定】]（笛吹市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（山梨県教育委員会・御坂町教育委員会・慈眼山南照院設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=原田道雄|year=1989|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 19 山梨県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490190|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}}} == 関連項目 == [[ファイル:井之上古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|井之上古墳出土 陶棺}}{{small|[[國學院大學]]博物館展示。}}]] * [[経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] * [[加牟那塚古墳]] * [[万寿森古墳]] * [[井之上古墳]] - 笛吹市御坂町上井之上付近で発見された古墳。石室内に陶棺を伴う。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:うはつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] 墳形は円形で、直径約40メートル・高さ10メートルを測る{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。埋葬施設は右片袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する。現存石室長17.54メートルを測る非常に大型の石室であり、山梨県では最大規模かつ東日本でも屈指の規模になる。盗掘に遭っているため副葬品は詳らかでない。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]末葉{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}（または6世紀後半）頃と推定される。南側には同時期の集落遺跡として二之宮・姥塚遺跡が広がる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。甲斐地方では古墳時代前期に[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（[[甲府市]]下曽根町）が築造されて以降、中期に大型古墳は築造されていないが、後期には本古墳のほか[[加牟那塚古墳]]・[[万寿森古墳]]（いずれも甲府市千塚）といった大型石室墳が築造されており、特殊な様相を呈する{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。なお伝承では、山姥が大男と競争して造ったため「姥塚」といい、[[聖徳太子]]が甲斐に入ったときに愛馬が倒れて葬ったので「御馬塚（おんばづか）」というとする史跡説明板。。 古墳域は[[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）に山梨県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1965年]]（[[昭和]]40年）5月13日、山梨県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1966年]]（昭和41年）、墳丘・石室の測量調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * [[1992年]]（[[平成]]4年）、南照院庫裏建設に伴う南側周溝部の発掘調査{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室パース図}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては右片袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 * 石室全長：17.54メートル（現存部） * 玄室：長さ9メートル、幅3.3メートル（奥壁部）・3メートル（玄門部）、高さ4.2メートル * 羨道：幅2.4メートル（玄門部）、高さ2.5メートル 石室の石材は1-1.5メートルの自然石で、乱石積みによって構築される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室の奥壁は2段積みで、下段は2枚組、上段は1枚石からなる{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。天井石は玄室部で5枚、羨道部で4枚（開口部の1枚は崩落）{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。玄室と羨道の境は、幅0.9メートル・高さ約2メートルの方柱状柱石で区画される{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 なお、南照院境内には大型の石材が点在することから、羨道はさらに5-6メートル長くなるとする説がある{{Sfn|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}。 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|俯瞰図 File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|開口部 == 文化財 == === 山梨県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 姥塚 - 1965年（昭和40年）5月13日指定[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚]（山梨県ホームページ）。[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html 姥塚【県指定】]（笛吹市ホームページ）。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（山梨県教育委員会・御坂町教育委員会・慈眼山南照院設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=原田道雄|year=1989|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1995|chapter=姥塚古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 19 山梨県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490190|ref={{Harvid|姥塚古墳（平凡社）|1995}}}} == 関連項目 == [[ファイル:井之上古墳出土 陶棺.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|井之上古墳出土 陶棺}}{{small|[[國學院大學]]博物館展示。}}]] * [[経塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] * [[加牟那塚古墳]] * [[万寿森古墳]] * [[井之上古墳]] - 笛吹市御坂町上井之上付近で発見された古墳。石室内に陶棺を伴う。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}} * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡03 > 姥塚] - 山梨県ホームページ {{デフォルトソート:うはつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]]

According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. {{Infobox Kofun in Japan |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ** Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:New pages]][[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[category:new pages]][[category:new pages]][[ja:姥塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site in Japan |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]][[ja:姥塚古墳]]

The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site in Japan |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:pages to try to fix ills]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]]

(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:original pages]] [[Category:kkk]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] ==References==

Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. {{Infobox ancient site |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[category:kk]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] ==References==

Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] ==References==

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka |part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |latitude = 35.634708 |longitude = 138.658292 |ISO = JP-19 |shape = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial_facility = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |excavated_artifacts = |satellite_kofun = |construction_period = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notable_features = |map2 = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=Ubazuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=38|lat_sec=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳}} and has been designated a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}}, represents a unique phase in the development of burial mounds in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was designated as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == Burial Facility == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} opening to the southwest. The dimensions of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Cultural Properties == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural Cultural Property Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round tumuli]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[category:unsorted completion categories]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ubazuka Kofun}}[[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] ==References==
