So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]]

The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|lt=kofun (tumulus)|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Fuefuki, Yamanashi|ja|笛吹市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fuefuki, Yamanashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|ja|聖徳太子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Shōtoku|qq=}} buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]]

The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|kofun (tumulus)]] located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]]

The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|kofun (tumulus)]] located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]]

Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]] [[Category:笛吹市の歴史]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|kofun (tumulus)]] located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki, Yamanashi]]

(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|kofun (tumulus)]] located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Ubazuka Kofun |image = [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and stone chamber entrance |alternate_name = Omabazuka | part_of = |location = [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan
(Within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple) |coordinates = 35.634708 |coordinates = 138.658292 |omitted = JP-19 |type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |size = Diameter 40m
Height 10m |burial = Single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |excavated_burial goods = |satellite_kofun = |built = Late 6th century |interred_persons = |mausoleum = |historic_sites = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site |tangible_cultural_properties = |notes = |map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=38|coordinates=4.95|lon_deg=138|lon_min=39|lon_sec=29.85|mark=円墳.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Ubazuka Kofun''' (姥塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|kofun (tumulus)]] located in [[:en:Fuefuki|Fuefuki, Yamanashi]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Japan. It is a {{ill|round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Ubazuka." == Overview == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), enkei.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main hall of Nanshō-in Temple (right) and Ubazuka Kofun (left)}}]] Located in the southeastern part of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōfu Basin|qq=}}, Ubazuka Kofun is a large round tumulus constructed at the edge of the Kanagawa alluvial fan. It is situated within the grounds of Nanshō-in Temple, where a statue of the deity {{ill|Kannon|ja|観音|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannon|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 観音菩薩}} is enshrined in its stone chamber. Surveys and excavations have been conducted on the tumulus and the surrounding area. The tumulus measures approximately 40 meters in diameter and 10 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Its burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The chamber, which is 17.54 meters long, is the largest of its kind in Yamanashi Prefecture and among the largest in eastern Japan. However, due to tomb robbery, details about burial goods remain unclear. The kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the late 6th century{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. In the surrounding area, the contemporaneous Ninomiya-ubazuka Archaeological Site suggests the existence of a settlement. Ubazuka Kofun, along with other large stone chamber tombs such as {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} and {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}}, represents a unique phase in the development of Kofuns in the region{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. According to legend, the mound was built as part of a contest between a mountain witch and a giant, hence the name "Ubazuka" ("Old Woman’s Mound"). Another story claims it was named "Omabazuka" ("Horse Mound") because [[:en:Prince Shōtoku|Prince Shōtoku]] buried his horse there during his travels in Kai ProvinceHistorical explanation board.. The kofun was Historic Site1 as a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site on May 13, 1965. == burial == [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-P.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Perspective view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Expanded diagram of the stone chamber}}]] The burial facility consists of a single-sided {{ill|yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} opening to the southwest. The area of the chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}: * Total chamber length: 17.54 meters (remaining portion) * Burial chamber: Length 9 meters, width 3.3 meters (rear wall) to 3 meters (entrance), height 4.2 meters * Passageway: Width 2.4 meters (entrance), height 2.5 meters The chamber is constructed with unshaped stones, measuring 1 to 1.5 meters each, laid in a rough masonry style. The rear wall of the burial chamber comprises two tiers: a lower tier of two large stones and an upper tier of one stone{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Five ceiling stones cover the burial chamber, while four stones (one of which has collapsed) cover the passageway. The entrance to the burial chamber is marked by a rectangular pillar stone measuring 0.9 meters in width and approximately 2 meters in height{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), 3D-B.jpg|Overhead view File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber (passageway direction) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-2.jpg|Passageway (toward the entrance) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), sendou-1.jpg|Passageway (toward burial chamber) File:Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki), entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Ubazuka - Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - Found in Fuefuki, featuring a {{ill|tōkan|ja|陶棺|1=Ceramic Coffins|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/tōkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceramic Coffins|qq=}} inside its stone chamber. == External Links == * [https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website) ==References== {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|version=99995710|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]][[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]]}} [[sn:Ubazuka Kofun]] [[ja:姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] [[ja:姥塚古墳]] [[sn:bazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fuefuki, Yamanashi]] {{Commons category|Ubazuka Kofun (Fuefuki)}}

Some researchers suggest that the passageway could extend an additional 5 to 6 meters based on the presence of large stones scattered around Nanshō-in Temple{{Sfn|Ubazuka Kofun (Heibonsha)|1995}}. Designated on May 13, 1965[https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki003.html#shi0029 Yamanashi Prefectural designation1 Guide: Ubazuka (Historic Sites)] (Yamanashi Prefecture official website).[https://www.city.fuefuki.yamanashi.jp/shisetsu/bunkazaihoka/046.html Ubazuka - Fuefuki City official website]. == Related Sites == * {{ill|Kamunazuka Kofun|ja|加牟那塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamunazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Inoue Kofun|ja|井之上古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} -
